bonuses.leadin: Smoke from industrial factories in Clichy can be seen in the background of one painting of this location. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Smoke from industrial factories in Clichy can be seen in the background of one painting of this location. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this location where a woman holds a monkey on a leash in another painting showing “Sunday Afternoon” on this location.
The Bathers was among five depictions of La Grand Jatte by this artist. This artist reproduced The Bathers for his manifesto Du Cubisme, written alongside Jean Metzinger (“met-san-ZHAY”).
Gleizes paid homage to Seurat again in Harvest Threshing, the largest Cubist painting along with this artist’s La Ville de Paris. This artist and his wife Sofia co-founded Orphism.
parts_sanitized: Name this location where a woman holds a monkey on a leash in another painting showing "Sunday Afternoon" on this location.
The Bathers was among five depictions of La Grand Jatte by this artist. This artist reproduced The Bathers for his manifesto Du Cubisme, written alongside Jean Metzinger ("met-san-ZHAY").
Gleizes paid homage to Seurat again in Harvest Threshing, the largest Cubist painting along with this artist's La Ville de Paris. This artist and his wife Sofia co-founded Orphism.
answers: Isle of La Grande Jatte [or Island of La Grand Jatte; or Île de la Jatte; or Île de la Grande Jatte]
answers_sanitized: Isle of La Grande Jatte [or Island of La Grand Jatte; or Ile de la Jatte; or Ile de la Grande Jatte]

bonuses.leadin: Orson Welles directed a 1974 documentary interviewing a Hungarian artist who created works with this characteristic. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Orson Welles directed a 1974 documentary interviewing a Hungarian artist who created works with this characteristic. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this characteristic of paintings like The Supper at Emmaus by Han van Meegeren. Elmyr de Hory created artworks with this characteristic.
De Hory's forgeries first came to light after he attempted to sell an alleged painting by this artist to the Fogg Museum. This Fauvist artist's legitimate works include The Joy of Life and The Dance.

John Myatt began forging art after his painting of a mountain village imitating this artist was accepted by Christie's. This artist of Portrait of Jacques Nayral wrote the treatise Du Cubisme with Jean Metzinger.
parts_sanitized: Name this characteristic of paintings like The Supper at Emmaus by Han van Meegeren. Elmyr de Hory created artworks with this characteristic.

answers_sanitized: forgeries [or F for Fake; or answers indicating fake art]

bonuses.leadin: The central figure in this work has a “24” embedded on his collar. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artwork, representative of its artist’s distilled Crystal Cubism era. Its central poilu is depicted playing the title game against a blue background.

Jean Metzinger collaborated with this artist and philosopher on the first theoretical treatise on Cubism. This artist’s La Chasse depicts a man with a golden hunting horn, with a group of hunting dogs to his right.
Metzinger was a co-founder of this artist collective associated with Cubism and Orphism. Josephin Peladan coined the primary name for this group due to his fascination with the quantity phi.
answers: Soldier at a Game of Chess [by Jean Metzinger]
answers_sanitized: Soldier at a Game of Chess [by Jean Metzinger]

parts_sanitized: Name this artwork, representative of its artist's distilled Crystal Cubism era. Its central poilu is depicted playing the title game against a blue background.

bonuses.leadin: The Cézanne retrospective at the 1907 iteration of this exhibition helped to inspire Analytical Cubism. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this annual art exhibition spearheaded by Frantz Jourdain in 1903. The term “Fauvism” was coined in response to its 1905 iteration.
In 1908, the Salon d’Automne infamously rejected this man’s painting Houses at l’Estaque, which led Louis Vauxcelles [voh-SELL] to coin the term “Cubism.”
This artist chronicled the fortunes of the Salon d’Automne in his The Epic: From Immobile Form to Mobile Form. This painter of Portrait of Jacques Nayral co-wrote the manifesto Du “Cubisme”.
answers: Société du Salon d’Automne [or Autumn Salon; do not accept or prompt on partial answer]
Albert Gleizes [glehz] (The other co-author was Jean Metzinger.)
answers_sanitized: Societe du Salon d'Automne [or Autumn Salon; do not accept or prompt on partial answer]
Albert Gleizes [glehz] (The other co-author was Jean Metzinger.)
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: The Cezanne retrospective at the 1907 iteration of this exhibition helped to inspire Analytical Cubism. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this annual art exhibition spearheaded by Frantz Jourdain in 1903. The term "Fauvism" was coined in response to its 1905 iteration.

This artist chronicled the fortunes of the Salon d'Automne in his The Epic: From Immobile Form to Mobile Form. This painter of Portrait of Jacques Nayral co-wrote the manifesto Du "Cubisme".

bonuses.leadin: Works in this art movement include La guitare and La Femme au Cheval. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this art movement pioneered by Georges Braque and Pablo Picasso in which things are painted or sculpted in an angular manner.
This man collaborated with Jean Metzinger on the book Du “Cubisme” as well as painting Man on a Balcony and Woman with Phlox.

Gleizes and Metzinger were key figures in this artistic group associated with the Cubist and Orphist movements. Sometimes called the Puteaux Group, they met in the house of Marcel and Jacques Duchamp.

bonuses.leadin: This artwork’s background depicts red mountains behind a lake that has a white sailboat in it. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this proto-cubist painting showing three title birds--including a pink flamingo--around a pond. It was painted by Jean Metzinger.
Jean Metzinger may be better known for co-authoring On Cubism with this other painter. This advocate of “crystal cubism” painted works like The Hunt.

Before Metzinger’s cubist period, he went through a divisionist phase, which is a form of this style with larger brush strokes. This branch of neo-impressionism was used in an 1884 painting depicting a monkey on a leash.
answers: Paysage coloré aux oiseaux aquatiques [or Colored Landscape with Aquatic Birds]
answers_sanitized: Paysage colore aux oiseaux aquatiques [or Colored Landscape with Aquatic Birds]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This artwork's background depicts red mountains behind a lake that has a white sailboat in it. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this proto-cubist painting showing three title birds--including a pink flamingo--around a pond. It was painted by Jean Metzinger.
Jean Metzinger may be better known for co-authoring On Cubism with this other painter. This advocate of "crystal cubism" painted works like The Hunt.

Before Metzinger's cubist period, he went through a divisionist phase, which is a form of this style with larger brush strokes. This branch of neo-impressionism was used in an 1884 painting depicting a monkey on a leash.

bonuses.leadin: This artist co-wrote a manifesto of Cubism with Jean Metzinger. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this painter of a portrait of Jacques Nayral, the almost entirely grey Woman with Phlox, and a painting called La Chasse, or The Hunt.

Gleizes’ painting The Bathers was exhibited at the 1912 edition of this salon, founded in 1884 in opposition to the government-sponsored Salon. In 1910, a painting by a donkey named Lolo was exhibited here.
This artist’s Bathers at Asnières was exhibited at the first Salon des Indépendants. This pointillist also painted A Sunday Afternoon on the Island of La Grande Jatte.
answers: Salon des Indépendants [or: Société des Artistes Indépendants, Salon/Society of Independent Artists]
answers_sanitized: Salon des Independants [or: Societe des Artistes Independants, Salon/Society of Independent Artists]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This artist co-wrote a manifesto of Cubism with Jean Metzinger. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this painter of a portrait of Jacques Nayral, the almost entirely grey Woman with Phlox, and a painting called La Chasse, or The Hunt.

Gleizes' painting The Bathers was exhibited at the 1912 edition of this salon, founded in 1884 in opposition to the government-sponsored Salon. In 1910, a painting by a donkey named Lolo was exhibited here.
This artist's Bathers at Asnieres was exhibited at the first Salon des Independants. This pointillist also painted A Sunday Afternoon on the Island of La Grande Jatte.
